240330.explanation: The gap between citizens and politics is already far too large. Cooperation is only worthwhile when it demonstrably adds value for the residents.

240331.explanation: Companies are good for the economy and employment of our residents.

240332.explanation: We support sustainable agriculture that benefits our residents.

240334.explanation: Our residents, who often wait many years for a home, should not be forced to make way for status holders.

240335.explanation: These costs should be paid by the national government, not by the residents of the municipality.

240336.explanation: Our community already provides a lot of shelter, previously at Camping Brugse Heide and still now for refugees from Ukraine. Precisely because we don't exaggerate, things have always gone well, and we want to keep it that way.

240339.explanation: Especially when it comes to verges, safety, and visibility.

240340.explanation: It yields little for the community and the costs quickly amount to 50,000 euros of public money.

240341.explanation: Companies should be encouraged to invest themselves in accessibility for their customers.

240343.explanation: Sports unite and contribute to social wellbeing; where needed, the municipality should help and possibly provide financial support.

240345.explanation: We must ensure that our residents can feel much safer than they currently do. But no extra enforcement officers that unnecessarily burden our entrepreneurs and residents.

240346.explanation: This contributes to safety, yet the police are hardly visible when you need them.

240347.explanation: Cameras should only be deployed if truly necessary for safety. Tailored solutions, not on every street corner.

240348.explanation: H&G stands for affordable housing aiming for 35% social rental. Especially on municipal lands, we can make a difference by realizing more social housing, so that other locations maintain space for a good mix for starters, families, and the elderly.

240349.explanation: We must be able to offer our youth and young families living space.

240351.explanation: On a voluntary basis; there are plenty of people willing and able to contribute.

240352.explanation: Young people are the future; that is where you invest.

240357.explanation: If there is a need and we can organize it safely and properly, why not?

240358.explanation: There are agricultural lands fragmented close to the centers of our villages. These lands will face many legal restrictions in the future, such as the use of crop protection near residential areas. Converting them to housing is therefore sensible, while large agricultural areas can be used for large-scale sustainable agriculture.

240359.explanation: If associations need our support, these associations and their members can count on us.

240360.explanation: It is a disgrace that this road exists, including the ambulance post. Fire brigade, police, and ambulance belong in a community.

240361.explanation: This helps young students from Valkenswaard.

240362.explanation: Not all tasks need to be tackled immediately. Sometimes capacity is spent on unnecessary matters while priorities lie elsewhere. Many civil servants do excellent work, but there are also tasks and methods that deserve a more critical review. Stricter management is justified there.

240363.explanation: A fine principle but not always feasible. For example: partly thanks to H&G, water nuisance on Eindhovenseweg and adjacent streets has been adjusted. A huge job replacing sewage from the center to Europalaan. We try to work in phases where possible, but inconvenience cannot always be avoided.

240365.explanation: First ensure decent, clean, and safe bicycle parking at bus stops and many other places in our village.

240368.explanation: This adds no value to our residents whatsoever.

240369.explanation: We at H&G have strongly advocated for a nice, cozy, and hospitable center, to which entrepreneurs contribute via this tax.

240370.explanation: It has always been like this. Also previously in the old Wedert, and that went well.

240372.explanation: But also to Maxima Medical Center where many residents must go daily, same route!

240375.explanation: Especially where the wellbeing of children is at stake, we should want that.

240376.explanation: Improve the Dorpsstraat but keep its authentic character.

240377.explanation: They can easily adjust the signage.

240378.explanation: These LGBTI people are simply part of our society and community life.

240380.explanation: A spearhead of H&G for years.

240330.explanation: VOTE wants Valkenswaard to keep control itself. No merger and no large A2 Cooperation. We only collaborate when truly necessary and it benefits residents.

240331.explanation: VOTE wants targeted promotion: SMEs and manufacturing fitting Valkenswaard. No large logistics halls. We prefer starters and high tech. The Paardenboulevard is also a strong showcase.

240332.explanation: VOTE believes we should strengthen existing nature and not expand it by converting farmland into nature.

240334.explanation: VOTE believes local residents should have priority for social housing. If national rules require it, we comply, but we make maximum space for starters and families from Valkenswaard.

240335.explanation: VOTE sees no need for extra municipal funds. The government covers the statutory task. We prefer to spend our money on housing, safety, and amenities, with good integration as a foundation.

240336.explanation: VOTE does not want a large asylum seeker center. We comply with the law through small-scale accommodation fitting Valkenswaard and that can be well supervised.

240339.explanation: VOTE believes public spaces should be tidy and pleasant. More frequent mowing is possible in busy places such as parks and playgrounds.

240340.explanation: VOTE thinks a central fireworks display is a wonderful moment for everyone. It contributes to vibrancy in the center.

240341.explanation: VOTE believes everyone should be able to participate. Therefore, we help entrepreneurs and associations where possible to make their premises accessible. Municipal buildings must also be well accessible.

240343.explanation: VOTE sees (sports) associations as a foundation of our society. The municipality can help with subsidies or suitable arrangements if really necessary.

240345.explanation: VOTE finds safety should be visible. More enforcement officers on the street help quickly address nuisance. We also want a clear reporting point so residents can report easily. This also helps prevent unreliable door-to-door sellers.

240346.explanation: VOTE finds short lines with the community police officer important. A weekly consultation hour per neighborhood facilitates contact. Together with enforcement officers, this provides quicker insight into problems and solutions.

240347.explanation: VOTE considers camera surveillance a drastic measure. It is only appropriate if there is a real problem and other measures do not work. We prefer visible enforcement and prevention.

240348.explanation: VOTE wants more affordable housing, but 40% social rental is too high and causes imbalance. We adhere to the 30% norm and aim towards 35%. Furthermore, we advocate for rental homes for middle incomes and affordable owner-occupied homes.

240349.explanation: VOTE believes we should build homes wherever possible, both inside and outside village boundaries. But smartly and fitting the village, with greenery and amenities.

240350.explanation: VOTE believes help should not come too late. By slightly raising the income threshold, we can also assist people just above the limit. This prevents bigger problems.

240351.explanation: VOTE values participation. Those who can contribute do so in their neighborhood or at an association. This helps build routine and strengthens social cohesion. With tailored support and guidance.

240352.explanation: VOTE considers youth work particularly important. It helps detect problems early and prevents young people from deteriorating. Cutting budgets costs more later. We prefer investing in guidance and activities.

240357.explanation: VOTE thinks hospitality venues should have space to keep Valkenswaard lively. Staying open later on weekends is possible, with agreements on noise, safety, and orderly departure.

240358.explanation: VOTE believes more housing needs to be built. Purchasing land can be done, but only if spatially the best choice, such as Lage Heide Zuid, Dommelkwartier, or the corner of Dorpsstraat/Bruggerdijk/Hoeverdijk.

240359.explanation: VOTE holds that sports must remain available. If a club is about to collapse, extra subsidy may be needed. We assess per club what is truly necessary.

240360.explanation: VOTE believes the police must be locally accessible. The police post in the town hall was a good pilot. We want it back so residents can easily contact the police and the police are visible.

240361.explanation: VOTE feels young people should have opportunities close to home. An MBO in Valkenswaard aids education for hospitality and manufacturing, with internships and apprenticeships. Good for youth and the economy.

240362.explanation: VOTE believes the municipality should work smartly. Digitalization and AI can speed up processes with fewer people. But we will maintain the right expertise in-house.

240363.explanation: VOTE wants roadworks to last as short as possible. If technically feasible, a road should not be closed for longer than a month.

240365.explanation: VOTE thinks an expensive parking facility for electric shared bikes is not yet needed. There is currently no problem with uncontrolled growth. If this changes, we will address it specifically.

240368.explanation: VOTE believes a nightclub can contribute to a lively center. That is only possible with good agreements on safety, noise, and closing times.

240369.explanation: VOTE wants less regulation for hospitality. Therefore, we will abolish terrace tax. This gives entrepreneurs space and makes the center livelier.

240370.explanation: VOTE considers the canteen of De Wedert should mainly remain a place for athletes and visitors. The canteen atmosphere and a free viewing area are important. Associations can play an important role in this. The municipality must maintain control over this.

240372.explanation: VOTE believes that you should be able to reach work and care well without a car. We lobby for better public transport connections to ASML, the High Tech Campus, and the hospital. This helps employees and reduces congestion.

240374.explanation: VOTE regards enforcement as skilled work. Safety requires well-trained people with clear rules and high integrity. Volunteers can act as 'eyes and ears', but not as enforcers.

240375.explanation: VOTE believes problems can be better addressed early. Couples therapy can help prevent a divorce and that is often better for children. We make help easily accessible with clear conditions.

240376.explanation: VOTE finds traffic safety important, but speed bumps are not always the best solution. We choose measures that really work against speeding and shortcut traffic, such as redesign and enforcement. The design will be consulted with residents.

240377.explanation: VOTE believes we must tackle shortcut traffic from Belgium. We consult with Lommel and Hamont-Achel to improve signage and make routes via Borkel less attractive.

240378.explanation: VOTE believes everyone should be able to be themselves. Inclusivity is part of our policy. We subsidize such activities but do not see this as a condition.

240379.explanation: VOTE believes residents should be able to have a say earlier. A citizens' assembly can help if the purpose and expectations are clear.

240380.explanation: VOTE focuses on free parking via blue zones in the center, with a maximum parking duration of three hours. This keeps parking accessible for visitors and encourages turnover, ensuring parking spaces remain available for shoppers and center users.

240330.explanation: Cooperation with the region is important and can increase our municipality’s strength. Together, for example, we have a larger budget and can realize projects that a municipality alone never could. Not less cooperation, but better and more transparent cooperation.

240331.explanation: GL-PvdA does not favor general marketing campaigns with public funds. However, we want the municipality to continue supporting Stichting Valkenswaard Marketing in targeted campaigns for more tourism, recreation, and employment.

240332.explanation: Agriculture and nature can coexist through extensive and nature-inclusive methods. Agricultural land freed up because farmers stop or relocate is preferably sold to surrounding farmers for sustainability. If that is not possible, nature development is a logical and valuable choice.

240334.explanation: Municipalities have a legal task to house status holders. Without housing there is no integration and no chance of work or school. At the same time, we recognize the pressure on the housing market and aim for extra affordable homes for all target groups.

240335.explanation: The municipality receives funds from the national government for housing status holders. Currently, leftover funds can be used for better integration. Such as investing in language learning, job finding, and better participation. Integration is important and desirable.

240336.explanation: A municipality cannot simply decide that a refugee center will never come. Solidarity between municipalities remains important. Currently, the focus is on housing status holders. Valkenswaard is open to a fitting, small-scale contribution, but not to a large-scale asylum center.

240339.explanation: Less mowing is better for biodiversity and better for the climate. GL-PvdA advocates ecological management and tailored measures per location. Playing fields must, of course, remain well accessible and therefore mowed often.

240340.explanation: The best New Year’s celebration is one we all celebrate together, with one big fireworks show organized by the municipality. This keeps it safe, animal-friendly, and allows us to enjoy a cozy New Year’s Eve together. Costs must be considered and there must be sufficient support.

240341.explanation: People with disabilities must be able to fully participate in society. But many important places are still not accessible enough, for example, for someone in a wheelchair. Entrepreneurs often cannot just afford such adjustments. Therefore, we believe the municipality should step in.

240343.explanation: Sports provide a lot of energy, fun, and satisfaction. It keeps you healthy and often the sports club is also a meeting place. If a sports club collapses, many residents suffer. Therefore, we believe the municipality should intervene if needed.

240345.explanation: BOAs (neighborhood enforcement officers) provide visibility and safety on the streets. They are approachable and assist the police with minor tasks. It is important they are well trained and that their tasks and authorities are clear.

240346.explanation: Visible and approachable neighborhood police officers are important for safety and trust. A fixed consultation hour makes contact with residents easier and helps detect problems early. Proximity is more important than a building. This also fits neighborhood-oriented work.

240347.explanation: Feeling safe on the street is very important. But more cameras are not the right solution in our opinion. Your privacy is a great asset that should not be violated lightly. Therefore, we prefer more street enforcement officers who can intervene immediately if something goes wrong.

240348.explanation: We prioritize affordable housing. The waiting list for social rental housing is far too long and the private market will not solve this. We aim for a ratio of 40% social rental, 40% middle rent and owner-occupied, and 20% free sector in every existing and new neighborhood.

240349.explanation: We prefer to build within the built-up area while preserving enough green space. We want to preserve the greenery around our town as well as possible. But to tackle the housing shortage, we may also have to use agricultural land for housing. That is also an opportunity to live in green surroundings.

240350.explanation: Many people with a low income currently fall just outside schemes making it hard for them to get by. Raising the threshold increases livelihood security, prevents debts, and works preventively against deeper poverty.

240351.explanation: People are not on welfare because they don’t want to work. They need a stable foundation, affordable housing, and trust from the municipality. Extra demands and conditions only create more uncertainty and cause new problems.

240352.explanation: Young people face pressure from all sides, from unaffordable housing to addictive social media algorithms. Through youth work, we prevent young people from (further) getting into trouble. In this way, we contribute to safe and strong neighborhoods where it is pleasant to live and grow up.

240357.explanation: GreenLeft-PvdA is open to longer opening hours on weekends to stimulate vibrancy and entrepreneurship. This can only be done with customization, proper enforcement, and clear agreements to ensure the livability and safety of residents.

240358.explanation: GreenLeft-PvdA is cautious about buying agricultural land for housing construction. First, all possibilities within the built-up area must be used. Only if that is insufficient can land acquisition be considered, maintaining landscape, nature, and agriculture. And then only on a voluntary basis.

240359.explanation: GreenLeft-PvdA considers sports important for health and social interaction. Subsidies don't have to automatically increase. Each club should be evaluated based on societal value and proper justification. Where necessary, the municipality can provide targeted support. The Youth Fund for Sports & Culture is available for youth and low-income individuals.

240360.explanation: GreenLeft-PvdA stands for safety close to home. Visible and approachable neighborhood police officers are more important than a building. Proximity increases trust and cooperation with care and the neighborhood. Reopening the police post is only meaningful if it contributes to this and is feasible.

240361.explanation: MBO (vocational) education is important for equal opportunities and the regional labor market. The municipality's role is mainly facilitating; a full MBO school is too costly and a national responsibility. Stimulating BBL courses (learning in practice) with local companies is desirable.

240362.explanation: Civil servants are needed for policy execution, service delivery, and enforcement. Having fewer civil servants often leads to poorer execution and more external hiring, which is more expensive than permanent staff. GreenLeft-PvdA chooses smart deployment and less bureaucracy but not blind budget cuts.

240363.explanation: Quality and safety take precedence over speed. Sometimes longer closures are unavoidable. However, we want better planning and communication about this so residents and others are well informed.

240365.explanation: Shared bicycles can ensure people use their cars less and reach places more easily, for example, cycling to Eindhoven. This must fit what residents need and want. Good parking facilities prevent nuisance and keep the streets neat and safe.

240368.explanation: A vibrant nightlife can contribute to a lively center and is important for young adults and tourism. At the same time, nuisance, safety, and traffic must be well balanced. A nightclub is only desirable if feasible and located with minimal impact on residents.

240369.explanation: GreenLeft-PvdA considers a lively center and strong local hospitality important. Using public space can cost something; full abolition of fees is not always fair. We also want to prevent uncontrolled growth of terraces. Reducing or temporarily waiving fees can sometimes be appropriate.

240370.explanation: The canteen at the De Wedert swimming pool performs a social function and must remain affordable and accessible. Taking over by a commercial party can lead to higher prices, limited opening hours, and mandatory purchase of refreshments. Therefore, the municipality should maintain control in consultation with the associations.

240372.explanation: A better public transport connection to major employers is needed. A direct bus line to ASML and the High Tech Campus reduces car traffic. Better public transport is crucial to shorten travel times and make public transport more attractive and reliable.

240374.explanation: Enforcement is the work for professionals. Volunteers do not have the proper authorities and training, which can lead to uneven rules and more conflicts. There is room, however, for neighborhood watch, where residents report problems to the municipality or police.

240375.explanation: Couples counseling is a form of prevention and can help prevent divorce problems, domestic violence, and entry into youth care. Because this is not covered by basic health insurance, municipal reimbursement for families at risk and with limited means can prevent a lot of social harm.

240376.explanation: Traffic safety and livability are paramount. Speed bumps help reduce speeding and make the Dorpsstraat in Borkel and Schaft safer. This is always done in consultation with the residents of the street and village.

240377.explanation: Borkel and Schaft experience a lot of nuisance from through traffic. The municipality must tackle this together with Belgian municipalities. However, a good analysis of the cause and extent of the problem is needed first to take appropriate and effective measures.

240378.explanation: Valkenswaard is a rainbow municipality and this requires ongoing commitment. Support for LGBTI activities helps with visibility, safety, and inclusion. Subsidies are given according to the usual rules, when a clear and substantiated application is submitted.

240379.explanation: Citizen assemblies help better involve residents in decisions. They create more support and engagement. They are especially useful for difficult and long-term issues. They complement the municipal council but do not replace it.

240380.explanation: Free parking can make the center attractive, but paid parking is necessary during busy times to prevent nuisance. There is already plenty of free parking in the center. We prefer people to come by bike. Paid parking generates revenue for other important facilities.

240330.explanation: CDA Valkenswaard opts for cooperation where needed and adds value. Regional cooperation is often indispensable to carry out tasks well and affordably. At the same time, the municipal council remains responsible for decision-making and oversight.

240331.explanation: CDA Valkenswaard supports targeted promotion to strengthen employment and a vital center, provided it is purposeful and goes hand in hand with investing in a good investment climate.

240332.explanation: CDA Valkenswaard wants to protect agricultural land. Farmers and rural areas deserve a future perspective. Nature development must be balanced with the operations and development of farms.

240334.explanation: CDA Valkenswaard believes the municipality is responsible for everyone living in Valkenswaard, but in case of severe housing shortage, clear choices must be made. We do not consider it fair to give priority to status holders in allocating social rental homes.

240335.explanation: CDA Valkenswaard finds good integration very important for social cohesion. Therefore, we must invest in language, work, and participation, with clear expectations for newcomers.

240336.explanation: We take our responsibility in asylum seeker reception but do so carefully, on a small scale and humanely, with attention for safety, livability, and support in Valkenswaard.

240339.explanation: CDA Valkenswaard values a well-maintained and safe public space, especially at playgrounds, roads, and residential areas. At the same time, we want to leave space for nature and biodiversity by mowing less often in other places.

240340.explanation: CDA Valkenswaard sees a municipal fireworks show as a good alternative to a complete ban.

240341.explanation: CDA Valkenswaard believes everyone should be able to participate in our municipality. Therefore, it supports targeted assistance so that smaller entrepreneurs can also make their premises more accessible to people with disabilities.

240343.explanation: CDA Valkenswaard is proud of its associations. Sports clubs are the glue of our society and also good for the health of our residents. Therefore, the municipality should support sports clubs when they encounter difficulties.

240345.explanation: CDA Valkenswaard supports deploying extra enforcement officers. Visible enforcement increases safety and livability in the municipality.

240346.explanation: CDA Valkenswaard supports proximity of the police but also recognizes capacity shortages. Where possible and feasible, contact with the neighborhood police officer is desirable.

240347.explanation: CDA Valkenswaard believes camera surveillance can contribute to safety, provided it is careful, temporary, and supplementary to human supervision.

240348.explanation: CDA Valkenswaard wants a balanced housing stock. A fixed percentage of 40% is high and can disadvantage other groups, such as starters and middle incomes.

240349.explanation: CDA Valkenswaard is open to housing construction outside village boundaries, especially through careful expansion at the edges of our cores. In developments in the countryside, we will be reserved and prioritize protection of landscape, nature, and farmers.

240350.explanation: CDA Valkenswaard prefers customization and temporary assistance over structurally raising income limits. Work must pay off.

240351.explanation: CDA Valkenswaard finds a reciprocal obligation reasonable, as long as it is appropriate and meaningful.

240352.explanation: CDA Valkenswaard sees youth work as a preventive investment in the safety and future of our youth. Youth work prevents problems and strengthens opportunities.

240357.explanation: CDA Valkenswaard is open to extending nighttime hours for the hospitality industry, provided livability and safety are well guaranteed.

240358.explanation: CDA Valkenswaard believes that with respect and careful land policy, farmers’ land can be purchased.

240359.explanation: CDA Valkenswaard strongly supports increasing subsidies for sports clubs. Several clubs are under pressure and just barely keeping afloat, so they deserve a municipality that helps.

240360.explanation: CDA Valkenswaard wants a recognizable point of contact within the municipality. Visible police presence increases safety and trust.

240361.explanation: CDA Valkenswaard advocates for an MBO school close to home, so young people can develop themselves and work locally.

240362.explanation: CDA Valkenswaard strives for a small government, but not at the expense of service delivery. Efficiency is leading in this.

240363.explanation: CDA Valkenswaard stands for realistic planning, even if it takes a little longer. Quality and safety come before speed.

240365.explanation: CDA Valkenswaard is open to sustainable mobility, provided there is demonstrable need and it is not a waste of money.

240368.explanation: CDA Valkenswaard believes Valkenswaard should be vibrant again. A nightclub can make a good contribution to making our nightlife livelier.

240369.explanation: CDA Valkenswaard wants to support the hospitality sector, but in a fair and financially responsible way. Reduction or targeted abolition is open for discussion.

240370.explanation: CDA Valkenswaard strives to deploy an independent hospitality entrepreneur in canteens, provided it ensures affordability, accessibility, and feasibility. Currently, this is not the case.

240372.explanation: CDA Valkenswaard supports urging the province for a direct bus line to ASML/High Tech Campus. This strengthens accessibility, reduces car traffic, and fits our regional cooperation.

240374.explanation: CDA Valkenswaard sees volunteers suitable for prevention, but not for enforcement. Enforcement requires professionalism and authority.

240375.explanation: CDA Valkenswaard values strong families, but reimbursing costs for couples therapy is, in our opinion, not a municipal task.

240376.explanation: CDA Valkenswaard aims to minimize the installation of speed bumps. We prefer visual speed reducing measures or road narrowings as solutions.

240377.explanation: CDA Valkenswaard supports cross-border cooperation to improve livability and traffic safety in our own municipality.

240378.explanation: CDA Valkenswaard believes everyone should be themselves, but must choose equal treatment and governmental restraint.

240379.explanation: CDA Valkenswaard finds it important to involve residents in important decisions because this increases support and trust in politics. A citizens' council can help, as long as it complements the elected municipal council.

240380.explanation: CDA Valkenswaard sees opportunities to use free parking as an impulse to make the town center more attractive.

240330.explanation: Specialist knowledge should be organized smarter and more effectively together. That increases quality and professionalism without unnecessary costs.

240331.explanation: An attractive location is created through good facilities and strong services, not through expensive promotion campaigns.

240332.explanation: We use space in Valkenswaard wisely. Not every area needs to remain agricultural land; we look at each location to determine what is best for our community.

240334.explanation: We want to offer appropriate shelter for status holders in non-independent housing. This helps newcomers get started and keeps social rental homes available for regular housing seekers.

240335.explanation: More money does not automatically lead to better integration. The initiative of newcomers themselves is crucial; participation works best when people take steps themselves. Smart choices are often more effective than bigger budgets, especially if extra spending comes at the expense of other facilities.

240336.explanation: As soon as the dispersion law expires, asylum reception in Valkenswaard will stop.

240339.explanation: We use municipal funds targeted: safety, housing, and a strong business climate have priority.

240340.explanation: Events are valuable for liveliness and social cohesion. The organization is primarily the responsibility of entrepreneurs and initiators.

240341.explanation: Accessibility is important and self-evident, with entrepreneurs having their own responsibility.

240343.explanation: Automatic financial support does not always help. We first look for solutions that are future-proof.

240345.explanation: More visible deployment of community enforcement officers ensures more calm, safety, and trust on the streets.

240346.explanation: The police maintain control over their own working methods; we respect that professionalism.

240347.explanation: Targeted camera surveillance can contribute to safety and solving incidents. Audio recordings go too far and we do not use them.

240348.explanation: Valkenswaard has a strong middle class. More than 30% social rental housing is therefore not necessary.

240349.explanation: We want to tackle the housing shortage and therefore must dare to look beyond the current village boundaries.

240350.explanation: Only more financial support is not a sustainable solution to poverty. We focus on self-direction and customized solutions so people truly gain perspective.

240351.explanation: Participating according to one's ability increases self-esteem, networks, and job opportunities.

240352.explanation: Our youth is the future. Therefore, we continue investing in youth work. We seek savings within the civil service.

240357.explanation: When entrepreneurs see opportunities, we will not stand in their way, within clear and predictable frameworks that prevent nuisance.

240358.explanation: Land acquisition is a task for project developers; the municipality sets frameworks and facilitates.

240359.explanation: Automatic financial support does not always help. We first look for solutions that are future-proof.

240360.explanation: The police maintain control over their own working methods; we respect that professionalism.

240361.explanation: The initiative lies with MBO schools; we actively think along with concrete plans.

240362.explanation: A more efficient organization leads to lower costs and more financial room for residents.

240363.explanation: Maintenance varies greatly in scope and complexity. Quality and safety are important, with good planning and as little inconvenience as possible.

240365.explanation: This way, we promote healthy transportation and give residents and visitors more freedom to move around easily and quickly.

240368.explanation: When entrepreneurs see opportunities, we will not stand in their way, within clear and predictable frameworks that prevent nuisance.

240369.explanation: Terraces bring liveliness; fewer burdens help our hospitality sector and strengthen the city center.

240370.explanation: Entrepreneurship belongs to entrepreneurs. The municipality focuses on its core tasks.

240372.explanation: Strong public transport connections improve accessibility to work and strengthen the socioeconomic opportunities of residents.

240374.explanation: Volunteer enforcement officers, such as with the police and fire brigade, can enhance safety and community engagement.

240375.explanation: The happiness and stability of children come first, over keeping parents together at all costs.

240376.explanation: We improve traffic safety with smart measures, without unnecessarily hindering motorists.

240377.explanation: Where there is no clear problem, we do not adjust traffic situations lightly.

240378.explanation: Everyone is equal. Existing facilities are accessible to all.

240379.explanation: On specific themes, a citizens' assembly can provide valuable insights, complementing the role of the municipal council.

240380.explanation: Paid parking contributes to good regulation.

240330.explanation: The major challenges of our time do not stop at municipal borders. Whether it concerns housing, energy, or mobility – cooperation at regional and national levels is essential. That does not mean that collaborations should not be regularly and critically reviewed.

240331.explanation: A promotional campaign for that is unnecessary. Investments in good mobility and energy facilities will truly make Valkenswaard more attractive for businesses.

240332.explanation: D66 wants to plant at least 20 hectares of forest by 2030. Wherever the opportunity arises to strengthen existing nature areas through agricultural land, D66 wants the municipality to seize that chance.

240334.explanation: Valkenswaard is a welcoming municipality: for all visitors and people fleeing war and violence. The municipality is obligated to house a number of status holders determined by the national government. Having a roof over one’s head is essential for quick integration in Valkenswaard.

240335.explanation: It is not just about money; it is about guiding newcomers as quickly as possible towards successful integration by supporting them with housing, education, and work. This may cost more money but also yields more for Valkenswaard and the newcomers.

240336.explanation: Valkenswaard is required under the distribution law to accept and house a number of asylum seekers (approximately 100 people) designated by the national government in cooperation with the COA. Valkenswaard is a welcoming municipality and D66 fully supports this law.

240339.explanation: D66 wants to strengthen nature. A restrained mowing policy offers insects more and better living space. This ultimately helps all flora and fauna.

240340.explanation: D66 supports a safe New Year's Eve for everyone and values beautiful traditions. We support new initiatives that can bring people together during New Year's Eve, with or without a professional fireworks or light show. The municipality should facilitate this but not organize it itself.

240341.explanation: Naturally, buildings must be accessible. We do not consider financially supporting entrepreneurs for this a municipal task. However, the municipality can advise entrepreneurs on how to make their buildings more accessible.

240343.explanation: D66 supports associations that have financial independence and a justified reason to exist. In unforeseen circumstances, a request to the municipality can always be made, which will then decide positively or negatively according to existing regulations.

240345.explanation: D66 sees no added value in more enforcement officers solely focused on safety. Extra supervision of parking and environmental offenses is desirable.

240346.explanation: D66 is in favor of better police visibility on the streets and in neighborhoods. If a weekly consultation hour contributes to that, we support it.

240347.explanation: D66 considers this an unnecessary infringement on the privacy of the residents and visitors of Valkenswaard. Therefore, D66 wants the municipality to be cautious with camera surveillance and only deploy it where a concrete (threat) situation makes it necessary.

240348.explanation: D66 is a strong supporter and advocate for a larger share of social rental housing among newly built homes. However, a minimum of 35% is sufficient to guarantee the right balance between homes for different target groups.

240349.explanation: By 2035, at least 2,000 new homes must be built in the municipality of Valkenswaard. These numbers cannot be achieved solely through densification within existing residential cores. Therefore, D66 also sees expansion possibilities in, for example, Lage Heide, Dommelkwartier, and Borkel & Schaft.

240350.explanation: D66 is satisfied with the current rules to support people in poverty. These also provide that if the current offered support (including financial aid) is insufficient, other options can always be called upon. This is subject to municipal assessment.

240351.explanation: Not in all cases. According to D66, social assistance is a (possibly temporary) customized provision. This means that for each benefit recipient, it must be considered to what extent a reciprocal obligation is possible or necessary.

240352.explanation: We want to offer youth more than currently available. Young people are and have the future. D66 therefore wants to invest in, for example, a youth center. Youth work has a very positive influence on liveability and safety in Valkenswaard today and tomorrow.

240357.explanation: D66 stands for a vibrant and safe nightlife in Valkenswaard. Extended opening hours for hospitality venues can be included if the entrepreneurs desire it and the liveability for local residents is safeguarded.

240358.explanation: D66 believes affordable housing is one of the most important challenges of our time. Agricultural land that could be purchased for housing in Valkenswaard is scarce, but where possible, D66 supports buying it. The municipality can then actively steer towards more affordable homes.

240359.explanation: D66 supports financially independent and autonomous sports associations. Our sports facilities already rank among the best in the Netherlands in quality and quantity, which is valuable but also costly. Spending even more is not appropriate.

240360.explanation: D66 favors better police visibility in Valkenswaard. Given the size of Valkenswaard, a police station is appropriate, even if it costs extra money.

240361.explanation: Funding for vocational schools (MBO) is provided by the national government. Only if there is a need for an MBO school or a branch thereof in Valkenswaard will D66 support this initiative and ask the municipality to facilitate and support it.

240362.explanation: Our civil servants do important work. D66 favors a 'lean' civil service apparatus that can perform the necessary work efficiently and effectively. Outsourcing is often more expensive and we generally consider it undesirable. However, we critically evaluate twice a year whether the staffing matches the tasks.

240363.explanation: D66 supports keeping roads closed for maintenance as short as possible. Unfortunately, this is not always possible due to the size or complexity of the work: then we choose the best long-term solution.

240365.explanation: D66 wants to realize the energy transition as well as possible and supports strengthening bicycle infrastructure. Shared bikes can contribute to this, especially if there is cooperation within the Brainport region. We want to invest in this!

240368.explanation: D66 supports a vibrant nightlife in Valkenswaard. If a nightclub can positively contribute to this, the municipality should cooperate.

240369.explanation: D66 supports a lively hospitality sector in Valkenswaard. Terraces are part of this. D66 supports levying a tax on terraces located on municipal and/or public grounds to cover the costs the municipality incurs for construction and maintenance.

240370.explanation: In D66's view, managing a canteen in the sports center De Wedert is not a municipal task. If possible, this should be taken over by an independent entrepreneur.

240372.explanation: D66 is a strong advocate for better and increased public transport. Bus routes to the Maxima Medical Center, ASML, and High Tech Campus would be a good addition as an alternative to car use.

240374.explanation: D66 does not support involving volunteers for enforcement tasks. However, D66 is in favor of engaging volunteers as hosts/hostesses to promote the livability of Valkenswaard.

240375.explanation: In D66’s view, funding couples therapy is not the municipality's responsibility. Mental health care belongs with the GGD/GGZ or other medical professionals.

240376.explanation: D66 supports a complete redesign of the road between the roundabouts on Maastrichterweg and Luikerweg, including implementing traffic-calming measures and separating the different traffic flows. The speed limit on the yellow road should be 30 kilometers per hour.

240377.explanation: D66 wants the public transport connections between Valkenswaard and Lommel & Hamont-Achel to be improved as an alternative to car traffic.

240378.explanation: D66 wholeheartedly supports Valkenswaard being a rainbow municipality and is eager to contribute to further developments. Initiatives that promote acceptance and emancipation can, in principle, be sponsored in the same way as other cultural or sports initiatives.

240379.explanation: D66 strongly supports the participation of Valkenswaard residents in making political decisions. Having a say increases acceptance of those decisions and often leads to better final results.

240380.explanation: D66 does not support the first 2 hours of free parking. It is simply the case that parking spaces in some parts of Valkenswaard’s center are scarce at certain times. Parking fees encourage conscious choices: the current restrictions are sufficient.

240330.explanation: The existing mandatory collaborations have too much influence. There is little control over these partnerships.

240331.explanation: This is important for the business climate.

240332.explanation: Unless legally required or if a natural area of equal size is converted into building land. Always in consultation with the farmer or livestock breeder.

240334.explanation: Priority can only be applied if there is sufficient housing available for our own residents.

240335.explanation: Newcomers should be well integrated and given more opportunities to do so. With additional guidance, better integration can be ensured.

240336.explanation: Asylum seeker centers do not contribute to a better municipality for our residents.

240339.explanation: The municipality should first adjust its maintenance policy. Due to greater biodiversity, it is advisable to implement a different mowing policy.

240340.explanation: A fireworks show as an alternative to private fireworks makes enforcing the fireworks ban easier. Additionally, a central fireworks show is a celebration for all residents.

240341.explanation: Entrepreneurs are personally responsible for good accessibility of their buildings. Care providers may be required to comply, and subsidies can be made available for this purpose.

240343.explanation: Mass sports are very important to the municipality of Valkenswaard. Offering as many residents as possible the opportunity to exercise or play sports. If a club has no future prospects, then no financial contribution will be given.

240345.explanation: This is necessary because the current capacity is mainly deployed in the center, and many violations in the neighborhoods are tolerated.

240346.explanation: On the contrary, the neighborhood police officer and enforcement officers must become even more accessible and visible.

240347.explanation: The traffic situation at the Market is often chaotic. Supervision and enforcement can make it safer.

240348.explanation: The current composition of Valkenswaard residents demands more social rental housing. For large projects, we certainly aim for at least 40% social rental. For small projects, this is not always possible.

240349.explanation: Only infill development is not sufficient. Expansion is necessary to meet the housing demand.

240350.explanation: It is also important that the same income limit applies for all support. Minimize administrative burdens.

240351.explanation: This depends on their capabilities. Ultimately, it contributes to the quality of life of others around them and the benefit recipient themselves.

240352.explanation: Young people deserve even more attention. A good youth is the best predictor of a good life.

240357.explanation: There is no reason to extend the current opening hours.

240359.explanation: Sports are important, especially for the youth.

240360.explanation: Although having a police station does not automatically result in more safety, it is important for the feeling of safety.

240361.explanation: This can provide a natural growth of young talent for our local industry and services.

240362.explanation: With the exception of Spatial Planning & Care Provision. By eliminating unnecessary rules, time can be freed up, so fewer civil servants are needed.

240363.explanation: Over the past 4 years, there has been a lot of large-scale maintenance. In the coming period, we want to limit that and allow it only for necessary sewer maintenance.

240365.explanation: This can only come at the expense of sidewalks, parks, or parking spaces. Encouraging electric shared bicycles results in dumped abandoned bikes on our streets. Notably, even in large cities providers suffer losses due to too little and/or improper use.

240368.explanation: For people between 18 and 25 years old, there is too little nightlife offering in our municipality. If entrepreneurs want to open a nightclub in the center, they are welcome.

240369.explanation: If our municipality really wants to be a recreational municipality and attract more tourists, then the community must also financially benefit. Otherwise, we will only experience nuisances.

240370.explanation: The municipality will never give enough freedom to an entrepreneur in the swimming pool to create a healthy hospitality business. Then all the risk lies with the entrepreneur, and that is not fair.

240372.explanation: This is free of charge, but also quite pointless. Our 'public' transport has been privatized, so this will only happen if it generates money. If it generated money, it would have happened already.

240374.explanation: Enforcement is different from assistance. It is a profession of respectful confrontation, not voluntary work.

240375.explanation: Parents with a good relationship have a strong impact on children growing up healthily. We can contribute to a good youth for more children by helping parents address relationship problems.

240376.explanation: A footpath must be realized as soon as possible so that residents can also move safely on foot.

240377.explanation: Free of charge and requires little effort. You can always try.

240378.explanation: People with a sexual orientation other than heterosexual are welcome in all associations, foundations, and organizations in Valkenswaard. In our opinion, special attention for this target group is not necessary. On the contrary, it increases the risk of exclusion.

240379.explanation: Valkenswaard Lokaal has always stood for more citizen participation, going beyond voting once every four years. Through our contribution, the opportunities for participation in municipal decisions have also been expanded, after they were reduced in previous periods.

240380.explanation: There is insufficient space to create free parking everywhere. To preserve parking spaces for people who need them, it is important to somewhat reduce car use in the center.

240330.explanation: The municipality should regulate more itself: local authority ensures locally focused decisions and strengthens democracy.

240331.explanation: By reducing regulatory pressure, applying relief measures, and simplifying permit applications, entrepreneurs will choose Valkenswaard themselves: the municipality does not need to set up expensive promotional campaigns for this.

240332.explanation: Protect food production and the village character.

240334.explanation: Priority for status holders displaces Valkenswaard residents who have been waiting for years; no preferential treatment.

240335.explanation: By implementing a local asylum stop, tax money for integration becomes unnecessary.

240336.explanation: Preserve the Valkenswaard identity, culture, and safety for the residents. No asylum seekers’ center in Valkenswaard.

240339.explanation: Mowing less saves costs, contributes to a more natural environment, and promotes biodiversity.

240340.explanation: A fireworks show on New Year's Eve replaces the tradition of setting off fireworks yourself. We oppose a private fireworks ban.

240341.explanation: Entrepreneurs have an interest in investing in accessibility themselves. The taxpayer should not have to pay for this.

240343.explanation: Sports clubs contribute to health, social cohesion, and local identity. This is important for a strong and vital municipality.

240345.explanation: Safety can be improved more efficiently by cooperating with the police, neighborhood initiatives, and smart prevention measures.

240346.explanation: Weekly consultation hours with the neighborhood police officer make the police more accessible and promote signaling, prevention, and engagement within the neighborhood.

240347.explanation: Camera surveillance in the center limits the privacy of residents and visitors. Safety can be better guaranteed through neighborhood watch and visible police.

240348.explanation: Mandatory 40% social housing limits the freedom of project developers, disrupts the housing market, and is counterproductive; it is not a solution to the housing crisis.

240349.explanation: Expansion threatens the village character and disrupts the landscape. Let’s first use the space within the village borders before considering expansion.

240350.explanation: Work must pay off again so that people can get by on their own and not depend on the government. However, municipal (poverty) policy must pay more attention to customization.

240351.explanation: There is no such thing as free money. Therefore, welfare recipients may well give something back to the community. Tailor-made solutions are essential!

240352.explanation: Parenting is the task of parents, not the government.

240357.explanation: The Valkenswaard hospitality industry is struggling; give entrepreneurs more freedom to operate and flourish.

240358.explanation: Preserve farmland for food security. Accelerate housing construction without sacrificing farmland.

240359.explanation: Increasing subsidies for sports clubs should not be the goal or starting point for the municipality. Extra support may only be granted if it is truly necessary and in the interest of the community.

240360.explanation: The police station in Valkenswaard must be reopened; local presence increases safety and involvement.

240361.explanation: Bringing an MBO school to Valkenswaard is important; it offers local education choices, binds young people to the region, and meets the demand for practically trained skilled workers.

240362.explanation: The municipality is hiring more and more people. This causes unnecessary bureaucracy and higher costs for taxpayers.

240363.explanation: Long-term closures cause unnecessary inconvenience and disruption for residents and entrepreneurs.

240365.explanation: No municipal storage for electric shared bicycles; the demand is too limited and the market can solve this perfectly by itself.

240368.explanation: Valkenswaard must return to its golden years: this village should become the region’s nightlife area again with a thriving center. A nightclub fits our hospitality vision!

240369.explanation: We lead the way when it comes to tax relief and reducing regulatory pressure. This way, we give entrepreneurs more space and stimulate the local economy.

240370.explanation: Entrepreneurs know best how to do their work; placing the hospitality sector under an entrepreneur stimulates improvement and initiative.

240372.explanation: A direct bus line to major workplaces will lead to an increase in expats and extra high pressure on the housing market. Eersel already suffers heavily from this and it is a lesson for Valkenswaard: we should not follow this path.

240374.explanation: Safety and livability are very important to us; we are open to exploring new solutions to improve this.

240375.explanation: Relationship therapy is for parents and possibly their insurance. This does not belong to the duties of the municipality.

240376.explanation: The Dorpsstraat in Borkel and Schaft is already a cobblestone road with speed bumps; speeding here is almost impossible. Extra speed bumps are unnecessary and hinder traffic. We choose for the motorist and against unnecessary obstacles.

240377.explanation: This not only hinders Belgian drivers but also residents of Valkenswaard. Bypass routes are crucial during closures and must remain free for local traffic.

240378.explanation: The taxpayer should not financially contribute to activities for any target group. Initiators will have to finance this themselves, for example through sponsorship or ticket sales.

240379.explanation: Residents of Valkenswaard must be able to participate in decision-making through referenda. A citizens' assembly is a form of token participation where the outcome is already predetermined. That has nothing to do with having a real say.

240380.explanation: Valkenswaard will further flourish by making the town center more accessible and attractive. Two hours of free parking directly contributes to this.

statements.title: The first two hours of parking in the center of Valkenswaard should be free.
The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
At least 40% of the new homes to be built in the municipality of Valkenswaard should be social rental housing. Currently, 29% of the homes in the municipality are social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in gemeente Valkenswaard moet minimaal 40% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In de gemeente is nu 29% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.moreInfo: The municipality is now aiming for 35 percent social rental housing in new construction projects. The minimum share of social rental homes in new projects of 10 homes or more is 30 percent.

statements.title: The municipality should ask the police to reopen the police station in Valkenswaard.
The municipality should handle more itself and participate less in partnerships with other municipalities.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should continue to give status holders priority for social rental housing.

titleParts.glossary: Refugees who have been granted a residence permit and are therefore allowed to stay in the Netherlands.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should urge the province of North Brabant to provide a direct bus line to major workplaces, such as ASML and the High Tech Campus.
Camera surveillance should be installed in the center of Valkenswaard.

titleParts.text: In het centrum van Valkenswaard moet

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should allocate funds to bring a vocational (MBO) school to Valkenswaard.
The municipality must allow a nightclub in the center of Valkenswaard.
There should be no asylum seekers' center (azc) in the municipality of Valkenswaard.

titleParts.text: In de gemeente Valkenswaard moet geen

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: The municipality should allow new construction outside the existing village boundaries.
The municipality must support more people in poverty by raising the income threshold for assistance.
statements.moreInfo: The municipality currently supports people with an income up to 130 percent of the social minimum. The social minimum is set nationally based on what a person needs for food, housing, clothing and participation in society.

statements.title: Subsidies for sports clubs should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

statements.title: The municipality of Valkenswaard should buy agricultural land for the construction of housing.
The municipality must abolish the tax on terraces at cafés and restaurants.
The municipality of Valkenswaard should start a pilot with volunteer enforcement officers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente Valkenswaard moet een proef starten met vrijwillige

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

statements.title: The municipality should invest in a storage/parking facility for electric shared bicycles.
The municipality should reduce the number of municipal employees.

titleParts.glossary: Spending less money.

statements.title: The municipality should spend more money on integrating newcomers into society.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet meer geld uitgeven aan

titleParts.glossary: Newcomers learn the language and customs of the Netherlands.
People who are new to the Netherlands.

statements.title: The municipality should place more speed bumps on Dorpsstraat in Borkel and Schaft.

statements.theme: Catering at De Wedert swimming pool
statements.title: The canteen of swimming pool De Wedert should be taken over by an independent operator.
statements.moreInfo: De Wedert swimming pool is owned by the municipality of Valkenswaard. At the moment the municipality manages the canteen itself.

statements.title: The municipality should subsidize activities for LGBT+ people.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.
LHBTI stands for lesbian women (L), gay men (H), bisexuals (B), transgender people (T) and intersex persons (I).

statements.title: The municipality should invest in a promotional campaign presenting Valkenswaard as an attractive location for businesses.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld steken in een

titleParts.glossary: A series of advertisements on, for example, billboards, television, and social media.
The location where a company establishes itself.

statements.title: If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

statements.title: The municipality should ask the Belgian villages Lommel and Hamont-Achel to take measures to stop through-traffic.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet aan de Belgische dorpen Lommel en Hamont-Achel vragen om maatregelen te nemen om

titleParts.glossary: Traffic that drives through residential streets to avoid busy roads.

statements.title: The municipality should mow public grass more often.
The municipality of Valkenswaard should reimburse couples therapy for couples with children.

titleParts.glossary: When people try to improve their romantic relationship together with a counselor.

shootoutStatements.title: Residents of the municipality of Valkenswaard should be able to have a say about political choices through a citizens' assembly.
Cafés in Valkenswaard should be allowed to stay open longer on weekends. Currently they close at 02:00 (Saturday morning) and 03:00 (Sunday morning).
The municipality should prevent agricultural land from being converted into nature areas.
The municipality of Valkenswaard should employ more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.
The municipality must ask the police to hold a weekly consultation hour with the neighborhood officer in every district.
The municipality should cut spending on youth work.
Roads in the municipality may not be closed for maintenance for longer than a month.
